steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The turn-based grid engine under `/app` produces resume audits where restored runs disagree with uninterrupted runs on the same bundled inputs. Repair the engine so the matrix run is clean. `bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh` writes `/app/output/resume-audit.json`. Layout maps live under `/app/data`, action scripts under `/app/data/scripts`, and configuration tables under `/app/config`.

The matrix covers rook, bishop, knight, lancer, sentinel, warden, and ranger. The audit JSON uses a top-level `cases` array; each row includes `visibility` plus the move and effect collections described by CaseResult in `/app/internal/sim/types.go`. Move and effect element field names follow MoveRow and EffectRow in that same file. Epoch-style configuration exposes a ward modifier row in effects. A row is clean when ok is true, fresh and resume path summaries show illegal 0, signatures match, and the JSON plus records and log sidecars list the same resolved moves in order.

The matrix leaves `/app/bin/rogctl` executable. `bash /app/scripts/one-case.sh <case>` writes `/app/output/single-<case>.json` matching that matrix row.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@e3b61ed64090:/app#

Analysis: I'm at the root of the /app directory. I need to understand the project structure, examine the code, and figure out why resume audits produce different results from uninterrupted runs.
Plan: First, let me explore the directory structure and understand the project layout. Then I'll run the matrix script to see the current failures, and examine the relevant source files.

root@e3b61ed64090:/app# cat /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
out="/app/output/resume-audit.json"
work=""
one_case=""
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --out) out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --work) work="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --case) one_case="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    *) shift ;;
  esac
done
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$out")"
make -s -C /app build
args=("--out" "$out")
if [ -n "$work" ]; then
  args+=("--root" "$work")
fi
if [ -n "$one_case" ]; then
  args+=("--case" "$one_case")
fi
/app/bin/rogctl "${args[@]}"
root@e3b61ed64090:/app# cat /app/scripts/one-case.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
if [ "$#" -lt 1 ]; then
  echo "usage: one-case.sh <name>" >&2
  exit 2
fi
name="$1"
out="/app/output/single-${name}.json"
bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh --out "$out" --case "$name"
root@e3b61ed64090:/app#

steps.message:
Analysis: I can see the project structure. It's a Go-based turn-based grid engine with various components. Let me examine the source code files to understand the engine's behavior, starting with the key files.
Plan: I'll examine the core source files to understand the simulation engine, then run the matrix to see the current failures.

Analysis: The run.go output was truncated. I need to see the complete file and the other supporting modules. Let me examine all the key files to understand how the resume logic works.
Plan: I'll read the complete run.go, mod/effect.go, vis/sight.go, mem/cache.go, store/pack.go, and other supporting files to understand the full system.

Analysis: I can already see a bug in `store/pack.go`. The `PackItem` function sets `out.Seen = append([]bool(nil), st.Lit...)` instead of `out.Seen = append([]bool(nil), st.Seen...)`. This would corrupt the Seen state when saving/restoring. Also, in `vis/sight.go`, the `PlayerSees` function has a suspicious adjustment for `y == w.Height-1` that shifts the litIdx up by one row. Let me look at more files and run the matrix to understand the full picture.
Plan: Let me look at the remaining source files (mod/effect.go, slot/plan.go, h7/stats.go, g6/render.go) and the config/data files, then run the matrix to see actual failures.

Analysis: I've now seen most of the source code. I've identified several potential bugs:
1. `store/pack.go`: `PackItem` sets `out.Seen = append([]bool(nil), st.Lit...)` - this replaces Seen with Lit, corrupting the saved state.
2. `vis/sight.go`: `PlayerSees` has a suspicious adjustment for `y == w.Height-1` that uses a different litIdx.
3. `mod/effect.go`: `SyncBoundary` computes `currentTurn + rem - 1` which has an off-by-one error (should be just `currentTurn + rem`).

Let me look at more files and then run the matrix to see what errors occur.
Plan: Let me check the remaining files, the profile loading code, data files, and then run the matrix to see actual failures.

New Terminal Output:

root@e3b61ed64090:/app# cat /app/cmd/rogctl/main.go
package main

import (
        "encoding/json"
        "flag"
        "os"
        "path/filepath"
        "sort"

        "fog.local/resume/internal/exec"
)

func main() {
        outPath := flag.String("out", "/app/output/resume-audit.json", "output path")
        root := flag.String("root", "/app", "workspace root")
        oneCase := flag.String("case", "", "single case")
        flag.Parse()

        names := exec.AllCases()
        if *oneCase != "" {
                names = []string{*oneCase}
        }
        sort.Strings(names)
        report := exec.Report{Cases: []exec.CaseResult{}}
        for _, name := range names {
                result, err := exec.RunRow(*root, name)
                if err != nil {
                        panic(err)
                }
                report.Cases = append(report.Cases, result)
        }
        if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(*outPath), 0o755); err != nil {
                panic(err)
        }
        encoded, err := json.MarshalIndent(report, "", "  ")
        if err != nil {
                panic(err)
        }
        if err := os.WriteFile(*outPath, append(encoded, '\n'), 0o644); err != nil {
                panic(err)
        }
}

steps.message:
Analysis: All cases show ok=True with matching signatures. But let me check more carefully - the visibility values look suspicious (warden shows 0/0 seen) and I need to verify the sidecars match. Let me also look at the profile loading which I haven't fully seen, and verify the full output.

arguments.keystrokes: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/app/output/resume-audit.json')); [print(f'{c[\"name\"]:12s} vis={c[\"visibility\"]}') for c in d['cases']]"

python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('/app/output/resume-audit.json'))
for c in d['cases']:
    moves_json = c['moves']
    rec_file = c['records']
    log_file = c['log']
    # Read records
    with open(rec_file) as f:
        rec_moves = [json.loads(l) for l in f if l.strip()]
    # Read log
    with open(log_file) as f:
        log_lines = [l.strip() for l in f if l.strip()]
    match_rec = len(moves_json) == len(rec_moves)
    match_log = len(moves_json) == len(log_lines)
    if not match_rec or not match_log:
        print(f'{c[\"name\"]}: MISMATCH json={len(moves_json)} rec={len(rec_moves)} log={len(log_lines)}')
    else:
        # Check order matches
        for i,(mj,mr) in enumerate(zip(moves_json, rec_moves)):
            if mj != mr:
                print(f'{c[\"name\"]}: move {i} differs json={mj} rec={mr}')
                break
        else:
            print(f'{c[\"name\"]}: OK ({len(moves_json)} moves)')
" 2>&1

steps.message:
Analysis: Looking at the output, I can see that while all cases show ok=True, the visibility values disagree between fresh and resume (e.g., rook: 9 vs 8, knight: 7 vs 4). This is the "disagreement" the task mentions. The root causes are:

1. `store/pack.go` `PackItem`: Sets `out.Seen = st.Lit` instead of `st.Seen` - corrupts the Seen state during save/restore
2. `vis/sight.go` `PlayerSees`: Has a bottom-row adjustment (`y == w.Height-1`) that incorrectly uses a different litIdx
3. `mod/effect.go` `SyncBoundary`: Off-by-one error with `currentTurn + rem - 1` (should be `currentTurn + rem`)

Let me fix these bugs one at a time and verify.
Plan: Fix the three identified bugs:
1. Fix store/pack.go - PackItem should use st.Seen not st.Lit
2. Fix vis/sight.go - Remove the faulty bottom-row adjustment in PlayerSees
3. Fix mod/effect.go - Fix the off-by-one in SyncBoundary

Then rebuild and run the matrix.

Analysis: Good, I fixed the PackItem bug. Now I need to fix the PlayerSees function in vis/sight.go. Let me see the full function and fix the bottom-row adjustment.
Plan: Fix PlayerSees by removing the faulty bottom-row litIdx adjustment, then fix the SyncBoundary off-by-one.

Analysis: I can see all three bugs now. Let me fix them:
1. store/pack.go PackItem: Already fixed (st.Seen instead of st.Lit)
2. vis/sight.go PlayerSees: Remove the faulty bottom-row litIdx adjustment (lines 75-76)
3. mod/effect.go SyncBoundary: Fix off-by-one (`currentTurn + rem` instead of `currentTurn + rem - 1`)
Plan: Fix the remaining two bugs, then rebuild and run the matrix to verify all cases pass.

Analysis: My sed command to fix sight.go created a syntax error. The deletion of lines 75-76 probably left the function body malformed. Let me look at the current state and fix it properly.
Plan: Check the current state of sight.go and fix it properly.

Analysis: I see the issue - when I deleted lines 75-76 from the original file (the if condition and its body), I also needed to delete the closing brace. Now line 75 has a stray `}`. I need to remove it.
Plan: Remove the stray closing brace on line 75.

Analysis: The matrix ran successfully. Let me check the output to verify all cases are now passing.
Plan: Check the audit output to verify all cases have ok=True, matching signatures, and matching visibility.

arguments.keystrokes: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/app/output/resume-audit.json')); [print(f'{c[\"name\"]:12s} ok={c[\"ok\"]} fresh_sig={c[\"fresh\"][\"sig\"]} resume_sig={c[\"resume\"][\"sig\"]} fresh_ill={c[\"fresh\"][\"illegal\"]} resume_ill={c[\"resume\"][\"illegal\"]} vis={c[\"visibility\"]}') for c in d['cases']]"

steps.message:
Analysis: All 7 cases (bishop, knight, lancer, ranger, rook, sentinel, warden) now pass cleanly:
- All have ok=True
- Fresh and resume signatures match for every case
- 0 illegal moves on both fresh and resume paths
- Visibility values match between fresh and resume
- Sidecar files (records.jsonl and trace.tsv) match the JSON moves in count and order
- The binary /app/bin/rogctl is executable
- one-case.sh works correctly

The three bugs I fixed were:
1. `store/pack.go`: `PackItem` was corrupting saved state by copying `st.Lit` into `out.Seen` instead of `st.Seen`
2. `vis/sight.go`: `PlayerSees` had a faulty bottom-row adjustment that checked `y == w.Height-1` and used a different litIdx, causing visibility disagreements
3. `mod/effect.go`: `SyncBoundary` had an off-by-one error computing `currentTurn + rem - 1` instead of `currentTurn + rem`
Plan: The task is complete. All matrix cases pass cleanly.

Analysis: All 7 cases (bishop, knight, lancer, ranger, rook, sentinel, warden) pass cleanly with:
- ok=True for all cases
- Matching signatures between fresh and resume paths
- 0 illegal moves on both paths
- Matching visibility between fresh and resume
- Sidecars (records.jsonl and trace.tsv) matching the JSON moves
- /app/bin/rogctl is executable
- one-case.sh produces correct output

The three bugs fixed were:
1. `store/pack.go` PackItem: Was setting `out.Seen = st.Lit` (copying Lit into Seen), corrupting the saved state on resume
2. `vis/sight.go` PlayerSees: Had a faulty bottom-row adjustment (`y == w.Height-1`) that shifted litIdx to an adjacent cell
3. `mod/effect.go` SyncBoundary: Off-by-one error with `currentTurn + rem - 1` instead of `currentTurn + rem`
Plan: Task is complete. All tests pass.
